name: Rottweiler
description: Descended from Roman drover dogs used to herd livestock across Europe, the Rottweiler was refined in the German town of Rottweil as a cattle herder and cart-pulling butcher's dog. When properly bred, socialized, and trained, Rottweilers are calm, confident family protectors with a deep devotion that often surprises new owners unfamiliar with the breed's gentle side. They require experienced, consistent handlers and early socialization to channel their natural guarding instincts in a controlled, appropriate manner.

conditions_detail.name: Hip Dysplasia
conditions_detail.description: Hip dysplasia is a genetic condition where the hip joint doesn't develop properly, causing the ball and socket to not fit together correctly. This leads to joint deterioration, pain, and arthritis over time.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Treatment ranges from weight management and physical therapy for mild cases to total hip replacement for severe cases. NSAIDs and joint supplements help manage pain.
prevention_tips: Screen breeding stock with hip scoring

conditions_detail.name: Elbow Dysplasia
conditions_detail.description: Elbow dysplasia is an umbrella term covering several developmental abnormalities of the elbow joint including fragmented coronoid process, osteochondritis dissecans, and ununited anconeal process. It causes foreleg lameness and arthritis.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Surgical intervention is often recommended to remove bone fragments or reshape joint surfaces. Medical management includes NSAIDs, joint supplements, and physiotherapy.
prevention_tips: Control growth rate in large breeds

conditions_detail.name: Osteosarcoma
conditions_detail.description: Osteosarcoma is the most common primary bone tumour in dogs, occurring predominantly in the long bones of large and giant breeds. It is locally aggressive and metastasises early, primarily to the lungs. Prognosis remains guarded despite treatment.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Limb amputation combined with chemotherapy (carboplatin or doxorubicin) gives median survival of 10-12 months. Limb-sparing surgery is possible in some cases. Palliative radiation and pain management are alternatives.
prevention_tips: Early investigation of unexplained lameness in large breeds over 5 years
